folio.label: 166v
folio.l:
if so be , æ æè fynde , æ by errour , or by other cause , harm or dama-
ge , may bitide ,

Also if thy cou~seil be dishoneste , or elles cometú
of dishoneste , cause , chaunge thy cou~seil ,

For the lawes seyn ,
æ alle byhestes , æ ben dishoneste , ben of no value ,

And eke if so
be , æ it be inpossible , or may nat goodly be ¹formed or kept ,

And
take this for a genäal reule ¢ That euäy cou~seil , æ is affermed so
strongly , æ it may nat be chaunged , for no condicion æ may betide
I seye , æ thilke cou~seil is wykked

¢ This Melibeus , whan he
had herd the docténe , of his wyf Dame Prudence , Answered i~
this wyse

¢ Dame q¶ he , as ©et in to this tyme , ©e han wel
& couenably taugút me , as in genäal , how I shal gouäne me , in æe
chesyng , & in the witúholdyng of my cou~seilloås ,

But now wolde
I fayn , æ ©e wolde co~descende in special ,

& telle me how liketú
©ow , or what semetú ©ow , by oure cou~seilloås , æ we han chosen in
oure päsent nede

¢ My lord q¶ she , I biseke ©ow in alle hu~blesse ,
æ ©e wol nat wilfully , replie ageyn my resons , ne distempre
©oure herte , thougú I speke thyng , æat ©ow displese ,

For god
wot , æ as in myn entente , I speke it for ©oure beste , for ©oure ho-
nour , & for ©oure ¸fyte eke ,

And sotúly I hope , æ ©oure benygnytee
wil taken it in pacience

¢ Trustetú me wel q¶ she , æ ©oure cou~seil
as in this cas , ne shulde nat , as to speke ¸prely , be called a cou~-
seillyngö , but a mocion or a meeuyng of folie ,

in whicú cou~seil , ©e
han erred , in many a sondry wyse

¢ First & forward ©e han erred
in the assemblyngö , of ©oure cou~seilloås ,

for ©e shulde first , han cle-
ped a fewe folk to ©oure cou~seil , & after ©e mygúte han shewed
it , to mo folk , if it hadde be nede ,

But certes ©e han sodeynly cle-
ped to ©oure cou~seil a gret multitude of peeple ful chargeant , &
ful a noyous for to heere

¢ Also ©e han erred , for æäe as ©e shulde
oonly han cleped to ©oure cou~seil , ©oure trewe frendes , olde & wyse

©e han I cleped straunge folk , ©onge folk , false flat äeres , & enemys
reconsiled , & folk æ doon ©ow reuäence , w outen loue

¢ And eke al-
so ©e han erred , for ©e han brougút w ©ow to ©oure cou~seil , Ire ,
Couertise , & hastifnesse ,

the whicú thre thynges ben contrious
to euäy cou~seil , honest & ¸fitable ,

the whicú thre thynges , ©e ne
han nat anientissed or destroyed he~ , neither in ©oure self , ne in
©oure cou~seilloås , as ©ow ougúte

¢ ©e han erred also , for ©e han
shewed to ©oure cou~seilloås , ©oure talent & ©ourö affeccou~ , to make
werre a noon , & for to do vengeau~ce ,

they han espied by ©oure
wordes , to what thyng ©e ben enclyned ,

& æäfore han æei cou~seilled
©ow , rathere to ©oure talent , than to ©oure ¸fit

¢ ©e han erred also
for it semetú æ ©ow suffisetú , to han ben cou~seiled by theise coun-
seilloås oonly , & witú litel a vys ,

where as in so gret , & so heigú
a nede , it hadde ben necessarie , mo cou~seilloås & more deliberacou~
to ¹forme ©oure empése
¢ ©e han erred also , for ©e ne han nat ex-
